name: Cinnamon Ice Cream
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine the half and half, cinnamon sticks, and vanilla bean in a heavy large saucepan. Scald over very low heat.
2. Remove from heat, cover, and let stand at room temperature for at least 1 hour.
3. In a large bowl of an electric mixer, combine sugar and egg yolks. Beat at high speed until the mixture forms a slowly dissolving ribbon when beaters are lifted.
4. Reheat the half and half over low heat.
5. Gradually beat 2 cups of the hot half and half into the egg yolk mixture. Stir in the remaining half and half.
6. Cook over low heat, stirring constantly, until a thermometer registers 180°F (82°C) and the custard coats the back of a spoon; do not boil. Remove from heat.
7. Immediately transfer the custard to a large bowl. Cool for at least 2 hours. If possible, cover and refrigerate for several hours or overnight.
8. Strain the custard through a fine-mesh sieve, discarding the cinnamon sticks and vanilla bean.
9. Whisk in the whipping cream. Transfer to an ice cream maker (in batches if necessary) and freeze according to manufacturer's instructions.
10. To serve, spoon into large bowls and garnish with fresh strawberries. Makes 1 gallon.
